Name: curvatureawareprecisionrouting
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Neural Network Training
Short Hypothesis: A curvature-aware precision routing framework can dynamically adjust numerical precision during training, improving both computational efficiency and numerical stability in neural networks.
Related Work: Existing work in mixed-precision training, such as Mix-GEMM (Reggiani et al., 2023) and QAOF (Almoudane, 2026), focuses on hardware-software co-design and layer-wise sensitivity analysis, respectively. However, these approaches do not dynamically adapt precision based on the curvature of the loss landscape. Recent work in curvature-aware methods, such as Curvature-aware Dynamic Precision for PINNs (Shao et al., 2026), suggests that precision sensitivity is phase-dependent. Our proposal extends this by integrating curvature diagnostics with precision routing to ensure both efficiency and stability.
Abstract: We propose a curvature-aware precision routing framework for neural network training, which dynamically adjusts numerical precision based on the curvature of the loss landscape. By leveraging spectral diagnostics and gradient covariance decay, our method ensures numerical stability while minimizing computational overhead. This approach integrates with existing optimization algorithms and is designed to be resource-efficient, suitable for single-GPU deployments. We validate our framework through empirical evaluations on a variety of neural network architectures, demonstrating improvements in both training efficiency and model accuracy.

Experiments.Name: Curvature Diagnostics
Experiments.Description: We will develop and implement spectral diagnostics to quantify the curvature of the loss landscape, including gradient covariance decay and Hessian conditioning. This will involve calculating the eigenvalues of the Hessian matrix and the covariance matrix of the gradients.
Experiments.Evaluation Metrics: Eigenvalue distribution, gradient covariance decay rate, Hessian condition number

Experiments.Name: Precision Routing Algorithm
Experiments.Description: We will design a precision routing algorithm that dynamically adjusts the numerical precision based on the curvature diagnostics. The algorithm will promote FP32 when the curvature indicates numerical stability and switch to FP64 when the curvature suggests numerical sensitivity.
Experiments.Evaluation Metrics: Training time, memory usage, model accuracy

Experiments.Name: Empirical Validation
Experiments.Description: We will conduct empirical validation on a variety of neural network architectures (e.g., ResNet, MobileNet, Vision Transformers) using standard benchmarks (e.g., CIFAR-10, ImageNet). We will compare our method against full FP32 and FP64 training, as well as static mixed-precision training.
Experiments.Evaluation Metrics: Training efficiency (time, memory), model accuracy, generalization performance

Experiments.Name: Ablation Study
Experiments.Description: We will perform an ablation study to evaluate the impact of each component of the precision routing framework, including the spectral diagnostics and the precision switching logic.
Experiments.Evaluation Metrics: Training time, memory usage, model accuracy

Risk Factors and Limitations: The dynamic precision switching may introduce additional overhead in terms of computational complexity and memory usage.
The precision routing algorithm may not always accurately predict the optimal precision level, leading to potential degradation in training efficiency or model accuracy.
The effectiveness of the curvature diagnostics may vary across different neural network architectures and datasets, requiring careful tuning and validation.
